description: Emerging semantic signals under watch for Vortik. These are not canonical anchors and should not be treated as protocol-stable registry primitives yet.

signals.name: Block-Level Access Lists
signals.summary: Execution-layer object under watch as Ethereum moves toward deterministic state access, execution predictability, and parallel execution.
why_it_matters: Signals movement toward concrete execution-layer terminology rather than broad market abstractions.
Supports deterministic state-access, execution predictability, and parallel execution research narratives.
May pressure broad labels such as execution market and blockspace market.

signals.name: Payload Timeliness Committee
signals.summary: Protocol-facing committee concept associated with ePBS timing, payload reveal accountability, and builder/proposer coordination.
why_it_matters: Directly relates to ePBS coordination and payload reveal timing.
Could become an important sub-surface around builder accountability.
Strengthens the need to track roles below the main ePBS primitive.

signals.name: Bid
signals.summary: Core ePBS-adjacent vocabulary around builder-proposer coordination and commitment-based block construction.
why_it_matters: Appears directly in the shift from auction framing toward commitment semantics.
Strengthens ePBS-related terminology.
Should remain contextual because it is too generic as a standalone ENS anchor.

signals.name: Payload
signals.summary: Core ePBS-adjacent vocabulary around block contents, reveal behavior, and builder-produced execution data.
why_it_matters: Payload terminology is central to ePBS and builder accountability.
It supports stronger semantic alignment around payload commitments and payload reveal.
Too broad to promote as a standalone registry anchor at this stage.

signals.name: Payload Reveal
signals.summary: Reveal-phase vocabulary associated with ePBS builder commitments, liveness risk, and payload timeliness.
why_it_matters: Directly connected to no-reveal and free-option risk.
Strengthens the execution hardening narrative around ePBS.
Best tracked as contextual vocabulary below the main epbs anchor.

signals.name: Execution Rights
signals.summary: Abstract authority related to execution payload contents, ordering behavior, or execution-adjacent coordination decisions. Still tracked below registry-anchor level pending stronger protocol convergence.
why_it_matters: Foundational abstraction behind execution tickets, future-slot markets, proposer delegation, and PBS evolution.
Stronger conceptual signal than many narrower auction variants.
Still too broad for anchor promotion and better tracked as an emerging abstraction until protocol-level convergence improves.

signals.name: Execution Rights Market
signals.summary: Generalized market surface for buying, selling, delegating, or allocating execution authority.
why_it_matters: May become a more precise successor framing to generic execution market language.
Connects execution tickets, slot markets, proposer rights, and execution delegation.
Should remain in emerging signals until terminology stabilizes further.

signals.name: Execution Tickets
signals.summary: Forward-looking execution coordination primitive involving transferable rights over future execution payload production.
why_it_matters: One of the stronger newer terms around future execution rights.
Could become important in post-ePBS execution-rights design.
Not yet treated as a canonical Vortik anchor.

signals.name: Slot Futures
signals.summary: Transferable claims or contracts tied to rights over future slots, including execution or sequencing authority.
why_it_matters: Distinctive and memorable future-slot terminology.
Intersects with execution tickets and proposer-rights markets.
Still dependent on broader execution-rights architecture.

signals.name: Execution Futures
signals.summary: Forward market structure where rights to future execution payload production are traded ahead of the target slot.
why_it_matters: Extends execution rights into temporal pricing and hedging.
Overlaps with execution tickets and slot futures.
Naming remains broad and should remain below registry-anchor level.

signals.name: Proposer Rights
signals.summary: Abstract set of rights associated with block proposal authority, including ordering, execution selection, and inclusion decisions.
why_it_matters: Reusable abstraction behind PBS evolution, delegation systems, and execution markets.
Widely understandable and increasingly stable as a conceptual layer.
Better as a conceptual signal than a primary anchor for now.

signals.name: Proposer Lookahead
signals.summary: Advance visibility into future proposers or proposer schedules before slot execution.
why_it_matters: Enables preconfirmations, delegation, and future-slot coordination mechanisms.
Foundational but not highly differentiated linguistically.
Importance depends on future consensus scheduling changes.

signals.name: Proposer Delegation
signals.summary: Delegation of proposer-associated rights such as sequencing, execution selection, or commitment issuance to external actors.
why_it_matters: Separates validator ownership from operational coordination functions.
Relevant across based sequencing, preconfirmation, and execution-rights designs.
Still broad and should not become a registry anchor without stronger convergence.

signals.name: Execution Commitments
signals.summary: Commitments tied specifically to execution behavior, inclusion, ordering, or execution-related guarantees.
why_it_matters: Separates execution guarantees from broader consensus guarantees.
Connects ePBS, preconfirmations, execution rights, and commitment-oriented infrastructure.
Overlaps with builder commitments and inclusion commitments.

signals.name: Builder Commitments
signals.summary: Commitments made by builders around payload production, inclusion, ordering, reveal behavior, or execution-related guarantees.
why_it_matters: Directly connected to ePBS reliability, payload reveal, and builder accountability.
Important bridge between commitmentlayer and epbs.
Naming remains crowded and should stay in watchlist.

signals.name: Inclusion Commitments
signals.summary: Explicit commitments guaranteeing that transactions will be included under defined constraints.
why_it_matters: Connects inclusion lists, preconfirmations, and commitment infrastructure.
Converts inclusion from best-effort behavior into a stronger coordination object.
Overlaps with FOCIL and preconfirmation terminology.

signals.name: Inclusion Auction
signals.summary: Auction-like mechanism specifically allocating guaranteed inclusion capacity or inclusion priority.
why_it_matters: More specific than generic execution auction language.
Connects inclusion rights with transaction guarantee systems.
Still not canonical and should remain a preparation signal only.

signals.name: Inclusion List Payload
signals.summary: Portion or representation of a block payload corresponding to transactions mandated by an inclusion list.
why_it_matters: Formalizes how inclusion constraints may materialize inside block construction.
Separates guaranteed inclusion from builder-selected transactions.
Naming is not yet converged enough for registry-anchor treatment.

signals.name: Payload Constraints
signals.summary: Constraint vocabulary describing rules or obligations attached to payload construction, reveal behavior, or inclusion enforcement.
why_it_matters: Connects ePBS payload construction with FOCIL-style inclusion enforcement.
May become relevant if inclusion constraints integrate directly into builder APIs.
Still emerging terminology and not yet a canonical object.

signals.name: Builder Obligations
signals.summary: Obligation vocabulary around builders complying with payload, inclusion, reveal, or execution-related constraints.
why_it_matters: Important for ePBS accountability and FOCIL interaction.
Signals a move from voluntary builder behavior toward explicit obligation semantics.
Could become important in builder-facing tooling and dashboards.

signals.name: Constrained Payloads
signals.summary: Emerging vocabulary for payloads subject to inclusion, ordering, reveal, or execution constraints.
why_it_matters: May connect FOCIL with ePBS payload construction.
Could matter if implementation language stabilizes around constrained payload construction.
Currently too early and too descriptive for anchor treatment.

signals.name: Execution Escrow
signals.summary: Escrow structure used to economically secure execution-related guarantees or commitments before final inclusion.
why_it_matters: Introduces enforceable collateralization into execution guarantee systems.
May connect to commitment enforcement and preconfirmation infrastructure.
Terminology is not yet standardized.

signals.name: Sequencing Rights
signals.summary: Abstract right to determine transaction ordering for a block, rollup, shared sequencer, or sequencing environment.
why_it_matters: Relevant across based sequencing, shared sequencing, delegation, and execution access systems.
Conceptually strong but not uniquely labeled.
May converge into a more specific canonical term later.

signals.name: Sequencing Commitments
signals.summary: Commitments governing transaction ordering behavior or sequencing guarantees across sequencing systems.
why_it_matters: Relevant to shared sequencing and ordering guarantees.
Overlaps with broader commitment systems.
Likely to remain implementation-specific unless naming converges.

signals.name: Based Sequencing
signals.summary: Rollup sequencing model where L2 ordering is anchored to L1 proposers rather than independent external sequencers.
why_it_matters: Important to rollup sequencing and Ethereum-adjacent ordering design.
May influence preconfirmation, sequencing, and proposer-linked coordination.
Relevant but external to the current L1 core registry.

signals.name: Based Preconfirmations
signals.summary: Preconfirmations issued under sequencing models anchored directly to Ethereum proposers rather than external centralized sequencers.
why_it_matters: Bridges based sequencing with transaction guarantees.
May become important if proposer-native low-latency coordination grows.
Terminology remains fluid and should not be treated as canonical yet.

signals.name: Flow Markets
signals.summary: Emerging umbrella term for competitive allocation of order flow, routing opportunities, sequencing access, or execution access.
why_it_matters: May capture the commercial layer around solver routing and order-flow infrastructure.
Still broad and not yet stable enough for anchor treatment.
Useful for tracking solverlayer and orderflowauction signals.

signals.name: Routing Markets
signals.summary: Emerging vocabulary around competitive transaction routing, wallet routing, solver routing, and execution access allocation.
why_it_matters: Relevant to wallet-routing providers, OFA systems, and solver coordination.
Could become commercially important even if not protocol-native.
Still too generic and unstable for registry-anchor treatment.

signals.name: Execution Allocation
signals.summary: Allocation vocabulary around execution access, execution opportunities, routing inventory, or execution rights.
why_it_matters: Connects solver coordination, routing markets, sequencing access, and execution rights.
May become an umbrella concept for modular execution infrastructure.
Currently interpretive and not a canonical registry anchor.

signals.name: LUCID
signals.summary: Encrypted-orderflow signal connecting inclusion guarantees, commit-before-reveal flow, and MEV-sensitive transaction protection.
why_it_matters: May strengthen the semantic importance of inclusion lists as a public-facing primitive.
May increase relevance of commitment-oriented vocabulary.
May pressure order-flow auction language if encrypted mempool terminology wins.

signals.name: Partial Block Auction
signals.summary: Auction mechanism applied to part of a block, especially where inclusion-list-constrained space coexists with competitive allocation of remaining block capacity.
why_it_matters: Connects inclusion constraints with residual block construction markets.
May become relevant if hybrid constrained/auctioned block construction models stabilize.
Naming remains unstable and should not be treated as canonical yet.

signals.name: Slot Auction
signals.summary: Time-based rights allocation surface where rights to propose, influence, or economically control a future slot may be auctioned ahead of time.
why_it_matters: May intersect with execution tickets or proposer-rights markets.
Captures a reusable coordination pattern around future slot control.
Terminology is ambiguous and should remain watchlist-only.

signals.name: Builder Fallback
signals.summary: Fallback path where proposers or protocol participants revert to alternative block-building behavior if external builders fail, censor, or do not reveal payloads.
why_it_matters: Relevant to PBS and ePBS robustness.
Connects builder failure, liveness, and censorship resistance.
Naming remains descriptive rather than canonical.

signals.name: Inclusion List Committee
signals.summary: Validator committee role associated with constructing or enforcing inclusion lists in FOCIL-like designs.
why_it_matters: Introduces a concrete role inside inclusion-list enforcement.
Separates inclusion enforcement responsibility from a single proposer.
Could become more important if FOCIL terminology continues to stabilize.

signals.name: Neutral Blockspace
signals.summary: Censorship-resistance and inclusion-oriented framing around transaction access to Ethereum blockspace.
why_it_matters: Adjacent to FOCIL and inclusion-list narratives.
May matter for public communication around censorship resistance.
Too broad and narrative-facing for registry-anchor treatment.

signals.name: Credible Neutrality
signals.summary: Normative and protocol-adjacent framing around censorship resistance, neutrality guarantees, and inclusion infrastructure.
why_it_matters: Strengthens the public narrative around inclusion lists and FOCIL.
Useful to track as a conceptual driver, not as a precise primitive.
Should not be treated as a standalone technical anchor.

signals.name: Gloas
signals.summary: Internal or implementation-facing terminology under watch around ePBS and fork-choice simplification discussions.
why_it_matters: May appear in client, testing, or consensus-spec discussions.
Not ENS-grade terminology at this stage.
Should remain watchlist-only unless it stabilizes publicly.

signals.name: Execution Safety
signals.summary: Execution-layer safety vocabulary connected to account abstraction, authorization behavior, delegation risk, and transaction execution guarantees.
why_it_matters: May become more important around EIP-7702-related security discussions.
Connects execution guarantees with user-facing safety constraints.
Too broad for canonical registry treatment.

signals.name: Authorization Constraints
signals.summary: Constraint vocabulary around delegated execution, account authorization, and transaction-safety boundaries.
why_it_matters: Potentially relevant to execution safety and account abstraction security.
May become important if authorization constraints become more formalized.
Currently contextual rather than registry-grade.

signals.name: Single Slot Finality
signals.summary: Finality roadmap concept under watch as SSF continues to pressure broader fast-finality language.
why_it_matters: May pressure fastfinality.eth if SSF becomes the dominant exact term.
Relevant to long-term Ethereum roadmap semantics.
Should remain watchlist-only unless the registry later adds a more exact SSF-aligned anchor.

signals.name: Key Provider
signals.summary: Potential future coordination role relevant to encrypted mempool or threshold decryption systems if protected order flow becomes more protocol-shaped.
why_it_matters: Could emerge as a meaningful role if encrypted transaction flow becomes more formalized.
Represents a possible semantic surface around protected order flow.
Still too early for registry-level treatment.

ignored_terms.name: Ranking Fee
ignored_terms.reason: Too narrow and design-specific. Better treated as internal mechanism vocabulary rather than a reusable semantic anchor.

ignored_terms.name: Inclusion Constraint
ignored_terms.reason: Conceptually valid but too generic. Better captured through inclusionlist and FOCIL.

ignored_terms.name: Escrowed Bids
ignored_terms.reason: Broad financial mechanism phrase with low Ethereum-specific naming distinctiveness.

ignored_terms.name: Execution Reservation
ignored_terms.reason: Mostly descriptive usage without canonical boundaries. More likely to remain implementation terminology.

ignored_terms.name: Execution Reserve Price
ignored_terms.reason: Mechanism detail and auction parameter rather than reusable semantic primitive.

ignored_terms.name: Inclusion Enforcement
ignored_terms.reason: Important concept, but too broad as a standalone term. Better captured through FOCIL and inclusionlist.
